title: Night Residential Care Worker
description:
Residential Care Worker will be working with the youths staying in an institutional facility, which focus on community rehabilitation.


They are required to work night shifts.


Fixed Monthly Allowance will be provided.


List of responsibilities, but not limited to:

- Oversee the residents on a daily basis; manage the welfare, discipline and standards of

regimentation in the institution;

- Support the shift during emergency;

- Deliver Trauma-informed practices in daily interactions;

- Ensure safety and security of premises, include perform body & property check, carry out security and night patrols, dormitory checks and conduct urine test (when necessary);

- Manage and provide care for the daily living needs of the residents in the Hostel;

- Able to develop positive relationship with the residents;

- Involve in taskforce, to assist and execute tasks required from the taskforce.


Technical Competencies

•Able to do rotating night shift.

•Independent, resourceful and responsible.

•Firm in upholding the standards and responsive in dealing with people.

•Possess good communication and writing skills.

•Proficient in MS Word, Excel, etc.


Core Competencies

• Resilience (Able to overcome adversity and emerge stronger);

• Integrity (Guiding decisions with moral principles);

• Serving (Willing to go the extra mile for others);

• Excellence (Always expecting the best of self);

•A good team player.


Preferred achivements

• O Level and above

postedCompany.description:
TRYBE LIMITED


Established in 1995, Trybe is a social service agency that specialises in working with youth facing adversities. Our vision is "Every Youth a Success Story". We believe that all youth are able to reach the fullest of their potential to live successful lives. Thus, we are united by our mission - to journey with youth who face adversities to lead purposeful lives; inspiring them to impact others.


Trybe, an Institution of a Public Character (IPC), is a member of the National Council of Social Service (NCSS). Our corporate values are resilience, integrity, service, and excellence.


Trybe runs various youth programmes and manages the Singapore Boys’ Hostel (SBHL). We provide youth with guidance and offer support for their families and communities, creating a complete ecosystem of care from community-based outreach and interventions, to individual in-care and aftercare services.
